summary: AIR-DROW Critical Input & Export Recovery Edition: hand gestures cannot download files, raster exports capture the visible artwork safely, and Kurdish mobile typography and protected UI spacing are refined.
features: Removes hand-triggered save/export downloads. Export now requires a real tap on an on-screen control.
Adds a reliable raster artwork snapshot path so PNG/JPG/WebP include visible drawing strokes instead of exporting an empty background.
Makes the hand guide opt-in by default, adds a longer palette hover dwell, and blocks drawing through the top palette.
Migrates existing project settings to the safer input defaults while retaining touch, pen, hand drawing and the two-finger hand eraser.
Refines Kurdish RTL text sizing, line-height and About-card layout for Android phones.

privacy.drawings: Your drawing data and preferences stay on your device unless you choose to export, share, or use an optional AI creation action.
privacy.camera: Camera permission is requested only after you choose to open the camera. Camera video is not saved by AIR-DROW.
privacy.handTracking: The deployed app uses the local same-origin hand model and runtime. It does not download a hand model while you draw.
privacy.checks: App readiness, hand drawing checks and local diagnostics read only browser/app state. They do not open the camera, upload a report, or include camera frames, hand landmarks, drawing points, project titles, or API URLs.

buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: The deployed browser loads the hand runtime and hand model from the same AIR-DROW site.
buildPolicy.delivery: The complete source package includes the verified hand model. The production build validates the local model before publishing.
